title: nastanthus andina
intro: Nastanthus andina is a plant species native to the Andes mountains, which is also known as the 'Andean mint' due to its mint-like properties. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of Nastanthus andina include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Nastanthus andina can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
